metadata.title: Mathematics graph (revised for atomic nodes and sharp relations)
notes: Summary nodes are used only as cluster hubs.
Node types are role-based: summary, claim, example.
Truth status and special roles live in metadata.
Gold revision adds explicit proof techniques, independence results, counterexamples, and theory-bridges.
Hub nodes are typed as 'hub' to diversify structural node labels without changing their role.
Component summary nodes are included so summarize_cluster supervision can compress mature theory branches.

nodes.text: Mathematics studies formal structure, quantity, and relation.
Foundations studies logic, proof, and set-based formalization.
Algebra studies abstract operations, structure, and linear systems.
Analysis studies limits, continuity, derivatives, and integrals.
Geometry and topology study shape, space, and structural invariants.
Number theory studies integers, primes, and arithmetic structure.
Probability and statistics study randomness, uncertainty, and inference.
Bridge insights connect mathematical ideas to computing, physics, and cryptography.
ZFC is a standard foundation for much of mathematics.
A consistent formal system rich enough for arithmetic has true statements it cannot prove.
Every mathematical statement can be proved true or false from one fixed axiom system.
The real numbers are uncountable.
Linear algebra studies vector spaces and linear maps.
A group has closure, associativity, an identity, and inverses.
Every non-constant complex polynomial has at least one complex root.
Matrix multiplication is commutative in general.
If v is an eigenvector of A, then Av = λv.
A limit is defined by the ε-δ condition.
The derivative is the limit of a difference quotient.
The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus links differentiation and integration.
A Taylor series expands a function around a point using derivatives.
Euler's identity is e^(iπ) + 1 = 0.
Euclid's parallel postulate is independent of the other Euclidean postulates.
In non-Euclidean geometry, triangle angles need not sum to 180 degrees.
Topology studies properties preserved under continuous deformation.
A manifold is locally Euclidean.
Every simply connected closed 3-manifold is homeomorphic to S^3.
A prime number has exactly two positive divisors.
Every integer greater than 1 has a unique prime factorization up to order.
For n > 2, a^n + b^n = c^n has no positive integer solutions.
All nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function have real part 1/2.
There are infinitely many prime numbers.
Probability is nonnegative, normalized, and additive on disjoint events.
Normalized sums of many independent variables tend toward a normal law under common conditions.
Bayes' rule is P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B).
A high correlation guarantees that one variable causes the other.
The normal distribution has density f(x) = 1/(σ√(2π)) · exp(-(x-μ)^2/(2σ^2)).
Earth's surface is locally flat but globally curved.
A coffee mug and a torus are homeomorphic.
RSA security relies on the difficulty of factoring large composite integers.
Differential equations connect analysis to physics.
Linear algebra underlies modern machine learning and computer graphics.
Modern public-key cryptography relies on number-theoretic hardness.
Curved manifolds provide the mathematical model for spacetime in general relativity.
Formal logic shaped theoretical computer science.
Proof by contradiction establishes a statement by assuming its negation and deriving an impossibility or inconsistency.
Cantor's diagonal argument constructs a real number missing from any proposed complete list, showing that the reals are uncountable.
Assuming ZFC is consistent, the Continuum Hypothesis can neither be proved nor disproved from ZFC alone.
All infinite sets have the same cardinality, so there is only one size of infinity.
Matrix multiplication is not commutative in general; changing the order can change the product or even make one product undefined.
If a square matrix has determinant zero, then it is not invertible.
If a function is differentiable at a point, then it is continuous at that point.
Every continuous function is differentiable.
There exist continuous functions that are nowhere differentiable, so continuity alone does not guarantee differentiability.
A space is simply connected when every loop in it can be continuously contracted to a point.
The Prime Number Theorem states that the number of primes up to x is asymptotic to x / log x.
Goldbach's Conjecture is the still-open claim that every even integer greater than 2 can be written as the sum of two prime numbers.
Under standard assumptions, sample averages converge toward the underlying expected value as the number of observations grows.
Conditional probability is defined by P(A|B) = P(A and B) / P(B) when P(B) > 0.
Statistical association alone does not identify a causal direction or rule out hidden common causes.
Foundational mathematics does not merely prove theorems; it also reveals independence phenomena and structured limits on what fixed axioms can decide.
Analysis links local smoothness information to approximation, showing how derivatives and series expansions capture nearby behavior.
Geometry and topology connect local structure to global shape by relating Euclidean neighborhoods, loop behavior, and whole-space classification.
Number theory links proved asymptotic laws for primes to deeper open conjectures about their fine distribution.
Probabilistic inference combines axioms, conditional probability, convergence theorems, and modeling assumptions; causal claims require more than raw correlation.
Foundational mathematics studies what formal systems can express, what they cannot settle, and how infinite collections differ in size and structure.
Algebra organizes operations and linear structure through groups, matrix behavior, invertibility, and spectral relations such as eigenvalues.
Analysis builds from limits to derivatives, continuity, integration, and local approximation by series.
Geometry and topology relate local Euclidean structure, continuous invariants, and global classification of spaces.
Number theory combines proved prime structure with asymptotic laws and still-open conjectures about how primes are distributed.
